title: Mountain, Sea and Desert Birding Tour
description: Explore Southern California’s rich birdlife on a guided journey from the mountains to the Salton Sea to the desert.  Join expert birders Gjon Hazard and Thomas Myers for an unforgettable birding experience as we explore and learn about the rich diversity of inland southern California (130+ species possible).    What to Expect:   Three days of guided birding across diverse habitats:    Friday: Salton Sea and the Imperial Valley    Saturday: Cuyamaca Rancho State Park    Sunday: Borrego Valley    Expert instruction on bird identification, ecology, conservation, and taxonomy    Daily meetups at the Anza-Borrego Foundation office with carpooling to each location    Opportunities for both rare and common bird sightings, guided by two of the region’s top birding experts    Easy to moderate, slow-paced walking with some uneven or rocky terrain    Variations in temperature, from hotter desert temperatures to cooler mountain mornings     Who Should Attend:   Birders of all levels—primarily beginner to intermediate, but all skill levels welcome     Nature lovers looking to deepen their understanding of regional birdlife    Anyone ready for three rewarding days outdoors in varied landscapes     What to Bring:   Binoculars    Lunch, snacks, and water each day    Comfortable clothing and shoes for outdoor conditions    Lodging arrangements (participants are responsible for their own accommodations)    Drivers should start with a (mostly) full tank of gas     REGISTER TODAY! Date & Time: (This event is three days)  Friday, November 20, 2026, 6:00 a.m.–3:00 p.m.   Saturday, November 21, 2026, 6:00 a.m.–3:00 p.m.  Sunday, November 22, 2026, 6:30 a.m.–3:00 p.m.     Location:    Meet at the ABF office in the Mall: 587 Palm Canyon Dr. #122, Borrego Springs, CA, 92004   (in The Mall, park in the eastside lot). We’ll then carpool and/or caravan to our birding locations.    Cost:   ABF Members: $213 (Become a Member for only $35 and save!)  Non-Members: $250  Students: $125     Cancellation Policy & Scholarships: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.      What Past Participants Have Said:     “Great group. Excellent guides, fun participants and great learning experience. Love the Mountain, Sea, Desert concept.”    “Great way to sample San Diego County's diversity in just three days.”    “Great locations- the guides really knew where to go”    “From beginning to the end of the 3 days, it was packed with good information, great guides, enlightening insights in the world of birds in the area. Loved the trip and the camaraderie. Gjon and Thomas were wonderful guides.”    “Learned about many great birding locations I wouldn't have known to visit with plenty of species, many of which were new sightings for me.”     About The Tour Leaders:  Gjon Hazard is a life-long California birder. He has led birding tours and field trips for the San Diego Bird Festival, Western Field Ornithologists, various Audubon societies, and other groups. Gjon is also a wildlife biologist with the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service where, for more than 20 years, he has worked with listed and at-risk plant and animal species. Prior to that, he conducted ornithological field surveys for the Smithsonian Migratory Bird Center, the U.S. Forest Service, academia, and biological consulting firms.      Thomas Myers has been birding for over 25 years and has led birding tours for Andiamo Travel (based out of Ensenada, Baja Norte Mexico). He has been working as a Wildlife Biologist for the San Diego Zoo, San Diego Natural History Museum, and other local consulting firms concentrating on field work with listed vertebrate species in the southwest including reptiles, amphibians, mammals, and, of course, birds!                                     how your support for nocturnal wildlife with the “I’m an Owl Ally” vinyl sticker, celebrating the owls that thrive beneath the star-filled skies of Anza-Borrego Desert State Park®, an official International Dark Sky Park. This bold design captures the mystery of desert nights, where owls silently patrol the moonlit landscape as vital members of the park’s ecosystem.

title: Astrophotography Workshop: Galleta Meadows
description: Capture the Cosmos in Borrego Springs   Unlock the secrets of astrophotography and learn how to capture stunning Milky Way shots in just one session! Join us just outside the Park in the breathtaking Galleta Meadows and discover the essentials of night sky photography, from planning to execution, in one of the best dark sky spots in the country.          What to Expect:       Hands-on astrophotography guidance in an iconic dark sky location         Step-by-step tips on camera settings, composition, and photo planning         Access to unique, easy-to-reach spots perfect for Milky Way shots         Personalized, small-group instruction to ensure individual attention         Minimal walking—just under 1/10 of a mile from parking to photo locations          Who Should Attend:       Beginners with a basic understanding of camera settings         Anyone eager to capture the beauty of the night sky         Photography enthusiasts looking to improve their astrophotography skills          What to Bring:       DSLR or mirrorless camera         Tripod and lens suitable for night photography         Sturdy closed-toe shoes         Head lamp          Get ready to elevate your photography game under the stars!        REGISTER TODAY!       Date: Friday, October 24, 2025     Time: 6:30 p.m.-8:30 p.m.     Location: Meet at the ABF office at 587 Palm Canyon Dr. #122 Borrego Springs, CA 92004 (in The Mall next to Kendall’s Café). He really set a great tone at the beginning of the workshop, and he adapted his approach to each student based on their needs. It was a really great workshop! I walked away learning a lot.”     “Great experience for my three-generation family”          About the Instructor: Chris Olivas is an award-winning landscape photographer and professional videographer. He was born and raised in San Diego, California. His education and photography have taken him all over the world. He also has been a science educator for over 14 years. He has an extensive background in astronomy and photography and has won numerous awards for his photography work. He loves the combination of science and art that astrophotography entails and loves to spread his passion for the night sky. He specializes in photographing the Milky Way in adventurous locations and sharing his experiences through educational workshops.

title: March Sunset Nature Photography Workshop With Paulette Donnellon
description: Discover the Art of Sunset Photography in Anza-Borrego  Join us for a one-of-a-kind opportunity to learn from award-winning photographer Paulette Donnellon as you explore the stunning beauty of the Anza-Borrego Desert at sunset.  What to Expect:   We’ll begin the afternoon at the Anza-Borrego Foundation Administrative Office, where Paulette will share her expert techniques for photographing desert wildlife, intricate plant life, and breathtaking landscapes.     She’ll then demonstrate her photo-editing process in Lightroom, with time for questions and discussion.    Afterward, we’ll caravan to a picturesque field location in time for sunset, where Paulette will guide you through shooting during the Golden Hour and Blue Hour, helping you refine your composition, optimize your camera settings, and frame that perfect shot.    You’ll also gain valuable insight into staying safe while exploring the desert, along with tips for photographing its rare and rugged beauty.    Who Should Attend:   Photography enthusiasts looking to improve their skills.    Nature lovers interested in capturing the desert's beauty.    Anyone interested in learning from a seasoned professional in a breathtaking, hands-on environment.     REGISTER TODAY!   Date: Saturday, March 14, 2026  Time: 3:30 p.m.- 8:30 p.m. (End time is approximate)  Location: Meet at the Anza-Borrego Foundation Administrative Office: 587 Palm Canyon Dr. #122, Borrego Springs, CA, 92004. This location is different from the State Park store and is located next to Kendall’s Café. We’ll then carpool and/or caravan to the field site in Anza-Borrego Desert State Park.     Cost:   ABF Member: $85  Non-Member: $100  Student: $50    Cancellation Policy & Scholarships: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.      What Previous Attendees Are Saying:  “…loved the location, the instructor, so knowledgeable and so talented! So passionate about the area and her photography!...Paulette is a treasure!”  “This was my first opportunity for such an event and I thoroughly enjoyed it. [Paulette’s] knowledge of the area plants and animals is invaluable my biggest take away was all the hitting hidden beauty in the desert!”  “I was able to see the beauty of the desert in a new way. Plus, with Paulette's help, I was able to capture the magic in photographs.”    About the Instructor:  Paulette Donnellon is an award-winning nature and wildlife photographer whose image of an Eastern Collared Lizard is featured on the 2024 America the Beautiful National Park Pass.  When she isn’t out in nature taking photos, she is employed by LEGO® Education and has served as a trustee for the San Diego County Board of Education for eight years and as an Executive Board member of Anza-Borrego Foundation. Paulette served nine years in the U.S. Army and was deployed during Desert Storm. She is incredibly knowledgeable about the flora, fauna and landscape features of Anza-Borrego. You can check out her photos on Instagram at @photogpaulette. title: Anza-Borrego Night Hike
description: Join us for a peaceful moonlit hike through the desert, where wide-open skies, rugged terrain, and the quiet beauty of the landscape come alive after dark.     What to Expect:      Under the glow of the moon and stars, we’ll experience the sights and sounds of the desert at night—from shifting shadows across the landscape to the subtle movement of nocturnal wildlife.     We’ll hike approximately 2-3 miles roundtrip across natural terrain.     Along the trail, you might see nocturnal creatures that thrive after dark, such as scorpions, bats, and owls.     This easy-paced night hike will be led by a wilderness first responder, ensuring a safe and fun experience for all ages.      Who Should Attend:      Nature lovers interested in experiencing the desert in a new and unique way.     Stargazers and those who appreciate wide, dark skies.     Hikers in moderate physical condition, comfortable walking several miles over sand or dirt trails.     Anyone looking for a memorable outdoor adventure at night.           REGISTER TODAY!         Date: Saturday, April 25, 2026     Time: 6:30 p.m.-9:15 p.m. (end time is approximate)     Location: Meet at the ABF store at 587 Palm Canyon Dr. #110, Borrego Springs, CA 92004 (in The Mall next to Calico’s). From there we’ll carpool/caravan to the trailhead (location to be determined).          Cost:     ABF Member: $25 Not a member? Join today for as little as $35 and save!     Non-Member: $30     Student: $15          Cancellation Policy:  Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.            What Past Participants Are Saying:      “Well organized. Educated and educating guides.”      “I appreciated the opportunity to be outdoors and experience the desert in a different way via a night hike.”      “It was a perfect evening under the stars”           Hike Leader:     Matt Nagy is a San Diego native who had the unique experience of growing up across the world. Before graduating high school, he lived in five different countries, including the USA. His global travels have given him the opportunity to see much of the planet, but without a doubt, San Diego County (especially Anza Borrego) remains his favorite place. In his free time, Matt enjoys long hikes, bike rides, and live music. When he has a few weeks off, he embarks on extended adventures such as river trips, bikepacking, or backpacking. Having spent many months under the stars, Matt is always planning his next adventure. He is excited to explore and appreciate the wonders of Anza Borrego with others, creating unforgettable memories along the way.

title: North County Nature Paddle Eco Tour
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us on one of our North County Kayaking adventures! Discover the natural beauty of Agua Hedionda Lagoon on a guided kayaking tour through this unique coastal ecosystem in Carlsbad. As you paddle through calm waters near the lagoon’s wetlands, keep an eye out for snowy egrets, great egrets, and great blue herons foraging along the shoreline. You may also spot a belted kingfisher darting across the water, killdeer along the muddy upland edges, or red-tailed hawks perched high on the powerlines above. Along the way, learn about local conservation efforts, wetland restoration, and the rich biodiversity that makes this lagoon such an important habitat. This eco tour is suitable for all experience levels and offers a meaningful way to connect with nature while supporting environmental education and stewardship. What to Expect: Wildlife Viewing: Watch for native birds and other wildlife as you explore the lagoon by kayak. Educational Insights: Learn about lagoon ecology, restoration projects, and sustainable aquaculture. Birding Support: Binoculars and bird identification guides are provided to enhance your experience. Guided Exploration: Enjoy expert-led interpretation while paddling through this peaceful marine habitat. Join us for an inspiring on-the-water experience highlighting the importance of preserving San Diego’s coastal resources. Group Discounts available on 6 or more tickets. Email eco-tours@oceanconnectors.org
location: California Watersports at Agua Hedionda Lagoon, 4215 Harrison St, Carlsbad, CA

title: Compost & Mulch Giveaway – 7/25 at Sun Runner Lot
partner_name: I Love A Clean San Diego
description: Join us for our upcoming Compost and Mulch Giveaway Event! This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY! Have you ever wondered what happens after you toss yard trimmings and food scraps into your green bin? The organic waste is collected and brought to the Miramar Greenery (located at the Miramar Landfill) where it is turned into compost! This compost is available at no cost to residents of the City of San Diego and can be picked up during normal business hours. To increase opportunities for residents to pick up compost, I Love A Clean San Diego and the City of San Diego are partnering to host Compost Giveaways on select weekends. Get your hands on some nutrient-rich compost for your garden at no cost. Whether you're a seasoned gardener or just starting out, this event is perfect for anyone looking to give their plants a boost. Swing by and grab some compost to help your garden thrive! Mulch is also available at this event. Things to bring: Shovel Gloves Containers Tarp and bungees for truck beds (ALL TRUCK BEDS MUST BE COVERED!) Things to know: Be prepared to self load compost and mulch All participants are REQUIRED to sign a release waiver Must approach from the EAST Exact Coordinates: (32.76726972788742, -117.20799017856716) Please be aware, in order to collect compost you are REQUIRED to sign a waiver. All participants will need to sign a waiver upon entering the event. About the Miramar Greenery: At the Miramar Greenery, compost is made from yard trimmings and food scraps that have been ground, placed in windrows, turned and watered for 70 days. During this time, microorganisms digest the carbon and nitrogen rich mixture, causing the windrow to sustain temperatures of 140-165 F. This process eliminates most weed seeds and pathogens while breaking down the organic material into beneficial soil nutrients. Finished compost is screened to a particle size of one-half inch or less which also removes film plastic from the final product. For more information, visit https://www.sandiego.gov/environmental-services/miramar Practical Application and Benefits : Compost can be used as an amendment to improve soil texture and increase nutrient and water holding capacity. In San Diego, where soil lacks many essential plant nutrients, mixing compost in with the soil is one of the best things you can do for your yard and garden. Note: This event will occur rain or shine, however if severe weather occurs the days leading up to the event, the event will be rescheduled or canceled. If needed, registrants will receive electronic communication as soon as possible before the event. This event is made possible by the City of San Diego Environmental Services Department and facilitated by I Love A Clean San Diego.
location: Sun Runner Lot, Sea World Dr & Pacific Hwy, San Diego, CA

title: Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us for a Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour! Join us for a guided Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour through the beautiful waterways of South San Diego Bay, ending in the restored Chula Vista Wetlands. This scenic, two-hour paddle offers a unique opportunity to explore coastal habitats while learning about the wildlife and conservation efforts that make South San Diego Bay so special. Along the way, you may spot osprey diving for fish, great blue herons and egrets wading in the shallows, nesting terns (April to September), and a variety of migrating birds (October to March). With a little luck, guests can even catch a glimpse of a Pacific green sea turtle cruising through the bay! This tour is welcoming to all skill levels and moves at a comfortable pace, with plenty of time to enjoy the surroundings. The route is about two hours long, and wind conditions can vary, adding a bit of adventure while still remaining accessible for beginners. What to Expect: Wildlife Viewing: Paddle through the waters, keeping an eye out for local wildlife, from native birds to coastal critters. Educational Insights: Our expert guides will provide fascinating information about the local ecosystems and conservation efforts. Chula Vista Wetlands: Paddle toward the restored wetlands, a vital area for wildlife and an important conservation success story. Come prepared for a fun and challenging adventure while experiencing the beauty of the Chula Vista Wetlands. Sign up for our next Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour today! Best of all, every tour supports our education programs for South Bay youth! Find more information at www.oceanconnectors.org Group Discounts available on 6 or more tickets. Email eco-tours@oceanconnectors.org
location: Bayside Park, 999 Bayside Pkwy, Chula Vista

title: Anza-Borrego Night Hike: Coyote Canyon
description: Venture into the desert after dark for a family-friendly night hike through Coyote Canyon’s Ocotillo Flat! We’ll watch the desert transform after sunset and explore the sights and sounds that make this landscape come alive under the stars.      What to Expect:      We’ll begin our adventure at Ocotillo Flats, surrounded by the silhouettes of ocotillo and creosote under the rising moon. We’ll hike roughly 3 miles roundtrip across gentle (though uneven) terrain.       Along the trail, we’ll look for nocturnal creatures that thrive after dark, such as scorpions.       This easy-paced night hike is led by an ABF staff member and certified Wilderness First Responder, ensuring a safe and fun experience for all ages.      Who Should Attend:      Families and friends looking for a unique way to enjoy summer in the desert.       Nature lovers eager to experience the desert at night and learn about its nocturnal life.       Hikers comfortable with a relatively easy 3-mile walk on sandy, uneven terrain.              REGISTER TODAY!            Date: Saturday, July 25, 2026     Time: 7:00 p.m.-10:00 p.m. (end time is approximate)     Location: Meet at the ABF store at 587 Palm Canyon Dr. #110, Borrego Springs, CA 92004 (in The Mall next to Calico’s). From there we’ll carpool/caravan to the trailhead.          Cost:     ABF Member: $28     Non-Member: $33     Student: $17     Cancellation Policy: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.           What Past Participants Are Saying:      “Great guides. Fun and informative. It wasn’t something I would have done on my own.”      “Well organized. Educated and educating guides.”      “I appreciated the opportunity to be outdoors and experience the desert in a different way via a night hike.”      “It was a perfect evening under the stars”              Hike Leader:      Born and raised between Thermal and the San Fernando Valley in California, Stacy Valdez’s journey is characterized by a blend of academic achievements, professional roles, and a profound dedication to social causes. Stacy has built a diverse and impactful career with a strong foundation in education and a commitment to community engagement and service. They have been involved in humanitarian efforts through Border Kindness since 2018, reflecting their passion for the outdoors, advocacy, language, and cultural studies. Their love for the environment began at an early age, growing once she moved to the San Fernando Valley, where she identified her love for the mountains and hiking and camping. Stacy is dedicated to promoting the outdoors to underserved youth and their communities.

title: The A, Bee, Cs of California’s Native Bees with National Geographic Explorer, Krystle Hickman (Webinar)
description: Join National Geographic Explorer Krystle Hickman for this presentation on native bees, their relationships with native plants, and community science engagement.      What to Expect:      Learn how California is home to over 1,600 species of native bees and how they are a vital part of the ecosystem.       Get to know more about these important pollinators through Krystle's stunning macro photography.       Discover what makes them special and how to support a healthy garden.          Who Should Attend:      Home gardeners and native plant enthusiasts interested in supporting pollinators        Nature lovers, photographers, and anyone curious about California’s native bees        Teachers, students, and environmental educators        Community science volunteers and conservation-minded residents        Beekeepers, master gardeners, and habitat restoration volunteers          REGISTER TODAY!        Date: Wednesday, July 29, 2026     Time: 12:00 p.m.–1:00 p.m.      Location: Online via Zoom. Instructions to access the webinar will be sent the week of the event.          Cost:       ABF Member: FREE     Not a member? Join today for as little as $35 and save!     Non-Member: $10      Student: $5      Cancellation Policy & Scholarships: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.            What Past Participants Are Saying:     “Depth, thoroughness and clarity of information as well as intriguing videos”     “I always eagerly anticipate upcoming virtual programs…they are terrific.”      “The presenter was articulate, well-paced, and really knew the topic.”         Bio:     Krystle Hickman is a National Geographic Explorer, community scientist, conservation photographer, and multi-award-winning author based in Los Angeles, California. Through her photography, she raises awareness about the decline of native bee species while highlighting the ecosystems they reside in.      Hickman’s work has taken her across the globe, where she documents native bees without using lethal collection methods. This approach has enabled her to document behaviors new to science and capture some of the first images of living representatives of many species.      Her work has been featured across television, digital platforms, and podcasts, including Ologies, and she has presented internationally, including at the 2024 United Nations Biodiversity Conference (COP16) in Colombia. Hickman has also lectured at institutions such as Harvard University, UCLA, and more.

Learn about our unique growing methods and how we cultivate food, community, and justice. The tour includes a hill and mixed terrain. We recommend wearing flat shoes, a sun hat, and bringing a water bottle. Learn about accessibility accommodations on the Farm. Pre-registration required. 🗓️ Sunday, August 9 🕒 10:30-12 p.m. | 📍Meet at the Coastal Roots Farm Coffee Cart 🎟️ $25/person. About Coastal Roots Farm We're nonprofit community farm and education center rooted in Jewish values and regenerative agriculture. We grow over 80,000 pounds of organic produce each year on our 18-acre farm, with more than 75% donated to community members experiencing food insecurity. Each year, we also welcome over 15,000 youth and adults for outdoor education, Jewish life programs, and community events that inspire environmental stewardship, climate action, and connection to Jewish tradition.

title: Compost and Mulch Giveaway – 8/15 at Los Penasquitos Ranch House
partner_name: I Love A Clean San Diego
description: Join us for our upcoming Compost and Mulch Giveaway Event! This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY! Have you ever wondered what happens after you toss yard trimmings and food scraps into your green bin? The organic waste is collected and brought to the Miramar Greenery (located at the Miramar Landfill) where it is turned into compost and mulch! These products available at no cost to residents of the City of San Diego and can be picked up during normal business hours. To increase opportunities for residents to pick up compost, I Love A Clean San Diego and the City of San Diego are partnering to host Compost and Mulch Giveaways on select weekends. Get your hands on some nutrient-rich compost for your garden at no cost. Whether you're a seasoned gardener or just starting out, this event is perfect for anyone looking to give their plants a boost. Swing by and grab some compost to help your garden thrive! Things to bring: Shovel Gloves Containers Tarp and bungees for truck beds (ALL TRUCK BEDS MUST BE COVERED!) Things to know: Be prepared to self load compost All participants are REQUIRED to sign a release waiver Must enter from the East (Black Mountain Road) Please be aware, in order to collect compost you are REQUIRED to sign a waiver. All participants will need to sign a waiver upon entering the event. About the Miramar Greenery: At the Miramar Greenery, compost is made from yard trimmings and food scraps that have been ground, placed in windrows, turned and watered for 70 days. During this time, microorganisms digest the carbon and nitrogen rich mixture, causing the windrow to sustain temperatures of 140-165 F. This process eliminates most weed seeds and pathogens while breaking down the organic material into beneficial soil nutrients. Finished compost is screened to a particle size of one-half inch or less which also removes film plastic from the final product. For more information, visit https://www.sandiego.gov/environmental-services/miramar Practical Application and Benefits : Compost can be used as an amendment to improve soil texture and increase nutrient and water holding capacity. In San Diego, where soil lacks many essential plant nutrients, mixing compost in with the soil is one of the best things you can do for your yard and garden. The County of San Diego's Rancho Los Penasquitos - In 1823, Francisco Maria Ruiz built a modest adobe that established Rancho Los Penasquitos as San Diego County's first Mexican land grant, remnants of which still stand within the present-day ranch house. Today, the surrounding park offers more than 10 miles of multi-use trails, a staging area, restrooms, and hosts weddings, reunions, and community events. The site also features the Los Peñasquitos Community Garden, founded in 2012, which provides 20 plots for local residents to grow their own produce through seasonal leases. The historic adobe serves as a cultural landmark and museum, offering visitors guided tours, educational programs, and a glimpse into the region’s early ranching and adobe life. For more information, visit https://www.sdparks.org/content/sdparks/en/park-pages/RanchoLosPenasquitos.html Note: This event will occur rain or shine, however if severe weather occurs the days leading up to the event, the event will be rescheduled or canceled. If needed, registrants will receive electronic communication as soon as possible before the event. This event is made possible by the City of San Diego Environmental Services Department and facilitated by I Love A Clean San Diego.
location: Los Penasquitos Ranch House, 12122 Canyonside Park Drive, San Diego

title: From Scraps to Soil: Vermicomposting Workshop

description: Build a worm composting bin for your home and learn the importance of diverting food waste from our landfills! Learn to reduce your food waste and compost at home! Join I Love A Clean San Diego for our free From Scraps to Soil: Vermicomposting Workshop at the Alta Vista Botanical Gardens on Saturday, August 22 at 10:30 a.m. During this workshop, we’ll cover: How to reduce food waste in the kitchen Why it’s so important to keep food waste out of landfills through composting and the organic waste recycling program Tips and tricks on how to use the curbside green bin Attendees can make and take home a vermicomposting bin (worm composting) during the workshop – all supplies and information will be provided to start easily composting at home! *Please note that supplies are limited to one bin per household, with priority given to residents of the City of Vista. description: The Ocean Conservancy and the California Coastal Commission partner with volunteer organizations, such as I Love A Clean San Diego to create the largest, international cleanup day. Join our efforts this year to be a part of this global event! This year, our countywide Coastal Cleanup Day will have around 100 + locations for volunteers to choose from, inland and coastal. Each site is hosted by a site captain (a specially trained volunteer) who will manage volunteers on site, properly dispose of the trash, and report back totals. title: September Anza-Borrego Night Hike
description: Join us for a peaceful moonlit hike through the desert, where wide-open skies, rugged terrain, and the quiet beauty of the landscape come alive after dark.  What to Expect:   Under the glow of the moon and stars, we’ll experience the sights and sounds of the desert at night—from shifting shadows across the landscape to the subtle movement of nocturnal wildlife.    We’ll hike approximately 2-3 miles roundtrip across natural terrain.    Along the trail, you might see nocturnal creatures that thrive after dark, such as scorpions, bats, and owls.    This easy-paced night hike will be led by a wilderness first responder, ensuring a safe and fun experience for all ages.   Who Should Attend:   Nature lovers interested in experiencing the desert in a new and unique way.    Stargazers and those who appreciate wide, dark skies.    Hikers in moderate physical condition, comfortable walking several miles over sand or dirt trails.    Anyone looking for a memorable outdoor adventure at night.      REGISTER TODAY!   Date: Saturday, September 26, 2026  Time: 6:00 p.m.-9:00 p.m. (start and end times are approximate and will be confirmed)  Location: Meet at the ABF store at 587 Palm Canyon Dr. #110, Borrego Springs, CA 92004 (in The Mall next to Calico’s). From there we’ll carpool/caravan to the trailhead (location to be determined).    Cost:  ABF Member: $28 Not a member? Join today for as little as $35 and save!  Non-Member: $33  Student: $17  Cancellation Policy: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.     What Past Participants Are Saying:   “Well organized. Educated and educating guides.”   “I appreciated the opportunity to be outdoors and experience the desert in a different way via a night hike.”   “It was a perfect evening under the stars”     Hike Leader:  Matt Nagy is a San Diego native who had the unique experience of growing up across the world. Before graduating high school, he lived in five different countries, including the USA. His global travels have given him the opportunity to see much of the planet, but without a doubt, San Diego County (especially Anza Borrego) remains his favorite place. In his free time, Matt enjoys long hikes, bike rides, and live music. When he has a few weeks off, he embarks on extended adventures such as river trips, bikepacking, or backpacking. Having spent many months under the stars, Matt is always planning his next adventure. He is excited to explore and appreciate the wonders of Anza Borrego with others, creating unforgettable memories along the way.              Don't forget a night sky guide before you head out!
location: 587 Palm Canyon Dr. #110, Borrego Springs, CA 92004

About the Miramar Greenery: At the Miramar Greenery, compost is made from yard trimmings and food scraps that have been ground, placed in windrows, turned and watered for 70 days. During this time, microorganisms digest the carbon and nitrogen rich mixture, causing the windrow to sustain temperatures of 140-165 F. This process eliminates most weed seeds and pathogens while breaking down the organic material into beneficial soil nutrients. Finished compost is screened to a particle size of one-half inch or less which also removes film plastic from the final product. For more information, visit https://www.sandiego.gov/environmental-services/miramar Practical Application and Benefits : Compost can be used as an amendment to improve soil texture and increase nutrient and water holding capacity. In San Diego, where soil lacks many essential plant nutrients, mixing compost in with the soil is one of the best things you can do for your yard and garden. The County of San Diego's Rancho Los Penasquitos - In 1823, Francisco Maria Ruiz built a modest adobe that established Rancho Los Penasquitos as San Diego County's first Mexican land grant, remnants of which still stand within the present-day ranch house. Today, the surrounding park offers more than 10 miles of multi-use trails, a staging area, restrooms, and hosts weddings, reunions, and community events. The site also features the Los Peñasquitos Community Garden, founded in 2012, which provides 20 plots for local residents to grow their own produce through seasonal leases. The historic adobe serves as a cultural landmark and museum, offering visitors guided tours, educational programs, and a glimpse into the region’s early ranching and adobe life. For more information, visit https://www.sdparks.org/content/sdparks/en/park-pages/RanchoLosPenasquitos.html Note: This event will occur rain or shine, however if severe weather occurs the days leading up to the event, the event will be rescheduled or canceled. If needed, registrants will receive electronic communication as soon as possible before the event. This event is made possible by the City of San Diego Environmental Services Department and facilitated by I Love A Clean San Diego.
location: Los Penasquitos Ranch House, 12122 Canyonside Park Drive, San Diego

title: November Sunrise Nature Photography Workshop with Paulette Donnellon
description: Capture the Magic of Sunrise in Anza-Borrego with Award-Winning Photographer Paulette Donnellon  Experience the desert at its most magical hour in this inspiring sunrise photography workshop led by Paulette Donnellon. Learn to capture Anza-Borrego’s breathtaking light, dramatic landscapes, and unique wildlife while gaining expert techniques to elevate your nature photography.  What to expect:   Learn professional tips for capturing the desert’s captivating wildlife, intricate plant life, and expansive landscapes.     Master timing for the Golden Hour and Blue Hour and refine your composition and cropping techniques.    Discover essential safety practices for desert photography and ways to photograph responsibly while protecting this fragile ecosystem.    Return to the ABF office for a post-session presentation, where Paulette will review the morning’s lessons and demonstrate photo-editing techniques to enhance your images.   Who should attend:    Photographers of all levels    Nature enthusiasts interested in landscape, wildlife, or plant photography    Those looking to perfect their photo editing skills    Anyone eager to explore Anza-Borrego Desert State Park through a photographic lens   REGISTER TODAY! Date: Sunday, November 1, 2026  Time: 5:00 a.m.-10:30 a.m. (End time is approximate)  Location: Meet at the Anza-Borrego Foundation Administrative Office: 587 Palm Canyon Dr. #122, Borrego Springs, CA, 92004. We’ll then carpool and/or caravan to the field site in Anza-Borrego Desert State Park.  Cost:   ABF Member: $90  Non-Member: $106  Student: $53  Cancellation Policy & Scholarships: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.    What Previous Attendees Are Saying:  “Paulette's photography workshop is worth the early rise as the desert wakes up into a blossom of color and beauty. It's a busy morning, bring everything you have as Paulette points out the shifting lights and colors.”  “This was my first opportunity for such an event and I thoroughly enjoyed it. [Paulette’s] knowledge of the area plants and animals is invaluable my biggest take away was all the hitting hidden beauty in the desert!”  “Paulette was very generous about sharing her expertise. Her suggestions for compositions in the field and her immediate feedback onsite were terrific. I appreciated her sharing post processing techniques as well. It was stunning to see the amazing range of color and beauty in the desert. I look forward to attending more workshops in the future. Thank you for an amazing day.”  About the Instructor:  Paulette Donnellon is an award-winning nature and wildlife photographer whose image of an Eastern Collared Lizard is featured on the 2024 America the Beautiful National Park Pass.  When she isn’t out in nature taking photos, she is employed by LEGO® Education and has served as a trustee for eight years for the San Diego County Board of Education and as an Executive Board member of Anza-Borrego Foundation. Paulette served nine years in the U.S. Army and was deployed during Desert Storm. She is incredibly knowledgeable about the flora, fauna and landscape features of Anza-Borrego. You can check out her photos on Instagram at @photogpaulette.                                     Keep cozy and stylish with this marbled green beanie, featuring a faux leather patch adorned with a vintage-inspired bighorn sheep design.     Ideal for cool mornings, hikes, or everyday wear, this beanie offers both warmth and a nod to the iconic bighorn sheep that roam the park’s rugged landscapes.

title: Intermediate Hike: Domelands
description: Journey through the sculpted wonders of Anza-Borrego  Explore the otherworldly terrain of the Domelands on this intermediate-level hike through Anza-Borrego Desert State Park. Discover wind and water-carved formations, slot canyons, and sweeping desert views that make this area a true geological treasure.  What to Expect:   Approximately 7 miles of moderate hiking with ~1,200 ft elevation gain, some uneven terrain, off-trail sections and light scrambling    Dramatic badlands, natural arches, and sculpted sandstone domes    Panoramic views of the Carrizo Badlands and Imperial Valley    A chance to see desert wildlife and seasonal blooms    Interpretive insights into the area’s unique geology and desert ecology   Who Should Attend:   Hikers with some experience looking for a moderate challenge    Nature and geology enthusiasts eager to explore off-the-beaten-path areas    Visitors eager to experience one of Anza-Borrego’s most fascinating natural features     REGISTER TODAY! Cost:  ABF Member: $30  Non-Member: $35  Student: $18    Cancellation Policy & Scholarships: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.  What Previous Attendees Are Saying about Our Hikes:  “I loved the hike choice and challenge. I will definitely be on another hike with the foundation soon!”  “I so enjoyed getting "off trail" and exploring with the Anza Borrego Foundation. The camaraderie, physical challenge, and education around Anza Borrego was beyond my expectations.”  “Expertise was great. cohesiveness of group was good. location excellent.”  Hike Leader:  In his professional life, Sergey Kuhsch has spent over 16 years in software development across different industries. In contrast, much of his free time is dedicated to exploring the natural world. As much as he enjoys hiking in chaparral or exploring the local mountains, he feels that Anza Borrego is a very special place. The sheer size and accessibility of the park are unlike any other in the region. The rugged landscape, endless vistas, and hidden oases offer unique opportunities to explore. Sergey feels that supporting the Anza Borrego Foundation is the most impactful way to support Anza Borrego Desert State Park.                                              You’re invited to explore the scientific detective story in San Diego’s diverse mountains and deserts spanning over 200 million years.  This includes the expansive landscapes of Anza-Borrego Desert State Park which is rich with ancient geological wonders.

title: December Anza-Borrego Watercolor Workshop
description: Connect with the desert and your creativity!  Unleash your inner artist in this hands-on watercolor painting workshop led by local artist Shirley Spencer. Whether you're a beginner or experienced painter, you'll learn how to beautifully capture Anza-Borrego’s unique landscape.  What to Expect:   Step-by-step instruction in watercolor techniques, composition, pigments, washes, design, and equipment usage    Inspiration from the desert’s natural beauty    A relaxed, creative atmosphere to explore your artistic side    All painting materials provided (optional to bring your own)    Take home your own desert-inspired masterpiece!    Casual, paint-friendly attire recommended   Who Should Attend:   Nature lovers with a creative spark    Beginner and experienced watercolor artists    Anyone looking to unwind and connect with the desert landscape through art     REGISTER TODAY!   Date: Sunday, December 6, 2026  Time: 9:30 a.m.—12:30 p.m.  Location: Steele/Burnand Anza-Borrego Desert Research Center (401 Tilting T Dr. Borrego Springs, CA 92004)    Cost:  ABF Member: $60  Non-Member: $71  Student: $36    Cancellation Policy & Scholarships: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.       What Past Participants Are Saying:  “Shirley was amazing. I've never seen one person give such a comprehensive introduction to Watercolor in such a short time. The location was beautiful and it was just a wonderful day to paint.”  “Shirley was a great instructor with a wealth of knowledge.”    Instructor:  Shirley Spencer earned her B.A. in Life Science and Biology from Pacific Union College and her Master’s Degree in Environmental Studies from Fresno Pacific University. She has published three books as an author and artist. Her book “Flowering Shrubs of Yosemite and the Sierra Nevada” was published by Heyday Books, with 40 of her original watercolors and scientific descriptions of each plant. “Our National Parks Alphabet Book” was published by Condor Designs to commemorate the 100th Anniversary of the National Park Service and includes vivid paintings of each park with endemic flora and fauna found in each iconic place. Her most recent book, “Living Among Giants; Botanical Treasures of a Sequoia Grove” was published in 2017 by Yosemite Conservancy. She divides her time between her winter haven in Borrego Springs, California and summer home in Fish Camp, California. title: Desert Trails: A Primitive Camping Adventure
description: Explore, Hike, and Camp Under the Stars in the Heart of the Desert  Get off the beaten path and discover the rugged beauty of the desert! This two-day adventure combines a scenic driving tour, invigorating hikes, and hands-on primitive camping skills for an unforgettable outdoor experience.  What to Expect:   Guided 4WD/AWD driving tour through Anza-Borrego Desert State Park    Two moderate-level hikes through stunning desert terrain    Learn primitive camping techniques—from setting up camp to outdoor cooking (we’ll provide a list of what to bring!)    Camp under a blanket of desert stars    Connect with fellow outdoor enthusiasts and nature lovers   Who Should Attend:   Adventurers eager to try primitive camping for the first time    Hikers and off-road explorers seeking a new challenge    Anyone who loves the desert and wants to learn wilderness basics     REGISTER TODAY!   Date/Time: 9:00 a.m. Saturday, December 12 – 12:00 p.m. Sunday, December 13, 2026    Location: Meet at the ABF Store (587 Palm Canyon Dr. #110, Borrego Springs, CA 92204). We’ll then carpool/caravan to the start of our driving tour.    Cost:  ABF Member: $102/person Not a member? Join today for as little as $35 and save!  Non-Member: $120/person  Student: $60/person    Cancellation & Scholarship Policy: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.      Tour Leaders:  Todd Linke is a resident of San Diego and just finished his 25th year in education, 23 of them with the Grossmont Union High School District. Todd grew up in Ohio and has been exploring, hiking and camping since he was a child. His earliest outdoor memories include hiking in the caves in Hocking Hills, sailing on Lake Atwood, swimming in Lake Erie, and camping at the seaside at Myrtle Beach.  Todd fell in love with the desert several years ago and makes the 2-hour drive to the park every chance he gets. He has visited the park over 100 times and has hiked and driven many of its well-known and more obscure trails and roads, but as always discovering more. Todd brings his love for science and his passion for the desert to any excursion in the park.       In his professional life, Sergey Kuhsch has spent over 16 years in software development across different industries. In contrast, much of his free time is dedicated to exploring the natural world. As much as he enjoys hiking in chaparral or exploring the local mountains, he feels that Anza Borrego is a very special place. The sheer size and accessibility of the park are unlike any other in the region. The rugged landscape, endless vistas, and hidden oases offer unique opportunities to explore. Sergey feels that supporting the Anza Borrego Foundation is the most impactful way to support AnzaBorrego Desert State Park.
location: Anza-Borrego Desert State Park / Meet at ABF Store (587 Palm Canyon Dr. #110, Borrego Springs, CA 92204)
